 {"id":"bd-26x8f","title":"Task: GIIL (iCloud image fetch) robot bridge","description":"## Overview\nExpose GIIL as a robot-friendly image fetch utility for remote debugging.\n\n## Deliverables\n- Tool adapter for giil (detect/version/health)\n- Robot command: ntm --robot-image-fetch --url=... (calls giil, returns local path + metadata)\n- Optional: integrate with Agent Mail to attach fetched images\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- Works over SSH/headless\n- JSON output includes saved path and mime","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-26y68","title":"Implement health classification engine","description":"Implement health classification engine for pane status.

## Status Categories

| Status | Detection Criteria |
|--------|-------------------|
| healthy | Agent process running, recent output (<5min), no error patterns |
| rate_limited | Output contains rate limit patterns (from bd-1mcvs) |
| unresponsive | No output for >5min AND process still running |
| crashed | No agent process OR zombie state |
| unknown | Cannot determine state |

## Implementation

Reuse status enrichments from bd-1g9wv:
- rate_limit_detected
- last_output_ts / seconds_since_output
- process_state
- child_pid

Aggregate into single classification per pane.

## Configuration

- UNRESPONSIVE_THRESHOLD_SECONDS: 300 (5 min)
- Allow override via flag or config

## Edge Cases

- New pane (no history): treat as healthy if process running
- Agent in long operation: distinguish from stuck","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:27:22.326210325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:27:48.663555396Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:27:48.663373874Z","close_reason":"Implemented as part of bd-31e1f diagnose.go - includes --diagnose-pane flag, overall health classification, --diagnose-fix auto-remediation, recommendation generator, and health classification engine","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-26y68","depends_on_id":"bd-31e1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-27gv","title":"Add --no-tui flag for scripting and CI environments","description":"## Problem
Dashboard may not work in headless/CI environments.

## Code Review Findings (2026-01-19)

### Partial Support Exists

Found terminal detection in multiple files:
- output/format.go:132-133 - `IsTerminal()` function
- output/success.go:71 - `isStdoutTerminal()`
- output/errors.go:52 - `isStderrTerminal()`
- cli/util.go:38 - Uses isatty library

### Missing: --no-tui Flag

No `--no-tui` or `--json` flags found for the dashboard command.

## Fix Implementation

### 1. Add Flags to Dashboard Command
```go
// cmd/ntm/dashboard.go
var dashboardCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "dashboard",
    Short: "Interactive dashboard",
    RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
        noTUI, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-tui")
        jsonOut, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")

        // Auto-detect non-interactive
        if !output.IsTerminal() || os.Getenv("CI") != "" {
            noTUI = true
        }

        if jsonOut {
            return runDashboardJSON()
        }
        if noTUI {
            return runDashboardPlain()
        }
        return runDashboardTUI()
    },
}

func init() {
    dashboardCmd.Flags().Bool("no-tui", false, "Plain text output (no interactive UI)")
    dashboardCmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "JSON output (implies --no-tui)")
}
```

### 2. Plain Mode Output
```go
func runDashboardPlain() error {
    status := getDashboardStatus()

    fmt.Printf("Fleet: %d/%d agents active\n", status.Active, status.Total)
    fmt.Printf("  Claude: %d  Codex: %d  Gemini: %d\n",
        status.Claude, status.Codex, status.Gemini)

    if status.Alerts > 0 {
        fmt.Printf("\nAlerts: %d critical, %d warning\n",
            status.Critical, status.Warning)
    }

    fmt.Printf("\nBeads: %d ready, %d in-progress\n",
        status.ReadyBeads, status.InProgressBeads)

    return nil
}
```

### 3. JSON Mode Output
```go
func runDashboardJSON() error {
    status := getDashboardStatus()
    return json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout).Encode(status)
}
```

## Unit Tests Required
```go
func TestDashboardNoTUIFlag(t *testing.T) {
    cmd := rootCmd
    cmd.SetArgs([]string{"dashboard", "--no-tui"})

    var out bytes.Buffer
    cmd.SetOut(&out)
    cmd.Execute()

    output := out.String()
    if strings.Contains(output, "\x1b[") {
        t.Error("--no-tui should not contain ANSI codes")
    }
    if !strings.Contains(output, "Fleet:") {
        t.Error("--no-tui should contain fleet status")
    }
}

func TestDashboardJSONFlag(t *testing.T) {
    cmd := rootCmd
    cmd.SetArgs([]string{"dashboard", "--json"})

    var out bytes.Buffer
    cmd.SetOut(&out)
    cmd.Execute()

    var status map[string]interface{}
    if err := json.Unmarshal(out.Bytes(), &status); err != nil {
        t.Errorf("--json output is not valid JSON: %v", err)
    }
}

func TestDashboardCIAutoDetect(t *testing.T) {
    t.Setenv("CI", "true")

    // Should auto-select plain mode in CI
    cmd := rootCmd
    cmd.SetArgs([]string{"dashboard"})

    var out bytes.Buffer
    cmd.SetOut(&out)
    cmd.Execute()

    output := out.String()
    if strings.Contains(output, "\x1b[") {
        t.Error("CI environment should auto-use plain mode")
    }
}
```

## E2E Test
```bash
# Test --no-tui
ntm dashboard --no-tui | grep -q "Fleet:" && echo "PASS" || echo "FAIL"

# Test --json
ntm dashboard --json | jq .fleet.total && echo "PASS" || echo "FAIL"

# Test CI mode
CI=true ntm dashboard 2>&1 | grep -q "Fleet:" && echo "PASS" || echo "FAIL"

# Test piping
ntm dashboard --no-tui | head -1 | grep -q "Fleet:" && echo "PASS" || echo "FAIL"
```

## Verification
- [ ] --no-tui flag outputs plain text
- [ ] --json flag outputs valid JSON
- [ ] CI=1 auto-detects plain mode
- [ ] Piped output works: ntm dashboard --no-tui | grep","notes":"Implemented --no-tui, --json, and --debug flags for dashboard command. Added shouldUsePlainMode() for auto-detecting CI environments, isTUIDebugEnabled() for NTM_TUI_DEBUG support. Plain text and JSON output modes now fully functional.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:13:49.112776980Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:08:33.816282755Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:08:29.329890849Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-27gv","depends_on_id":"bd-2g6y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-27gv","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-27hn0","title":"EPIC: Privacy & Secrets Safety (Redaction + Privacy Mode)","description":"## Summary
NTM orchestrates many AI agents and therefore routinely handles **high-risk text**: API keys, OAuth tokens, credentials, secrets in env/config, and sometimes PII in logs/prompt history/pane scrollback. Today, individual features occasionally mention redaction (e.g. checkpoint export), but NTM lacks a **single, consistent, centrally-tested redaction + privacy policy layer**.

This epic adds a unified **Secrets/PII safety pipeline** and a first-class **Privacy Mode** so users can confidently use NTM in sensitive repos without accidental leakage via:
- `ntm send` / palette prompts
- `ntm copy` / `ntm save`
- checkpoints / exports
- Agent Mail messages
- REST/WS/web dashboards (as they mature)
- event logs, prompt history, archives

## Goals
- Provide one canonical Go implementation for **detection + redaction** with strong unit tests.
- Make safe behavior easy: sensible defaults + ergonomic flags and config.
- Ensure **no silent data loss**: redact intentionally; when blocking, explain clearly.
- Ensure robot outputs remain stable and machine-readable.

## Non-goals
- A full enterprise DLP product.
- Perfect secret/PII detection (we want high-signal, low false positives; allow overrides).

## Proposed Design (high level)
- Introduce a small internal package `internal/redaction` that:
  - detects sensitive strings (provider keys, JWTs, common secret patterns; optional high-confidence PII)
  - supports deterministic redaction (stable placeholders)
  - supports modes: `off | warn | redact | block`
  - supports allowlisting + extra patterns (per-project overrides)
- Integrate the pipeline at all high-risk IO boundaries (see child features/tasks).
- Migrate existing checkpoint export redaction (`internal/checkpoint/export.go` `--redact-secrets`) to delegate to `internal/redaction` (see bd-32svm) so we do not maintain multiple regex lists.

## Success Criteria
- A user can enable privacy/redaction via config or flags and be confident that:
  - clipboard/file outputs and logs do not leak raw secrets
  - automated exports (support bundles/checkpoints) are safe-by-default
  - errors explain what was blocked and how to override safely
- Comprehensive tests exist (unit + E2E) with clear structured logging.

## Notes
- Prefer standard library crypto + regex; avoid new deps unless unavoidable.
- Keep implementation incremental: land library + one integration point, then expand.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:19:44.323969164Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-01T04:09:40.099701527Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
